Details: Both outputs address Snowflake to BigQuery conversion testing but with significantly different levels of detail and sophistication. Basic_Test_Document provides 6 fundamental test cases with simple pytest implementation, while Comprehensive_Test_Document offers 10 comprehensive test cases including edge cases, error handling, and performance testing with advanced pytest fixtures and helper functions.

Both outputs target the same core objective of validating Snowflake to BigQuery SQL conversion. They share 5 common test scenarios: JSON field access (TC001/TC01), array aggregation (TC002/TC02), type casting (TC003/TC03), window functions (TC004/TC04), and semi-structured data filtering (TC005/TC05). However, Comprehensive_Test_Document extends beyond with additional edge cases (TC02-TC05, TC07-TC10) and error handling scenarios (TC08-TC09) not covered in Basic_Test_Document. The semantic intent alignment is strong for overlapping areas but diverges in scope and depth.

Fundamental structural differences exist between the outputs. Basic_Test_Document uses simple text-based test case format with basic pytest functions, while Comprehensive_Test_Document employs professional tabular structure with detailed columns (Test Case ID, Description, Preconditions, Test Steps, Expected Result, Actual Result, Pass/Fail Status). The pytest implementations differ significantly: Basic_Test_Document has straightforward test functions with basic assertions, while Comprehensive_Test_Document includes sophisticated fixtures (@pytest.fixture), helper functions (setup_test_data, teardown_test_data), parameterized testing approach, and comprehensive SQL query integration.
